clues.clue: In 'Toy Story,' name the space-ranger action figure who becomes Woody's friend.
In 'Monsters, Inc.,' this short, one-eyed green monster is Sulley's coach and best friend.
In 'Up,' name the friendly dog whose special collar voices his thoughts aloud.
Voiced by Phyllis Smith, this blue, bespectacled emotion appears in 'Inside Out.'
This Pixar director helmed 'Toy Story' (1995) and later led the studio creatively.
This 1991 Klasky-Csupo series followed a band of babies led by Tommy Pickles.
This football-headed boy lived in his grandparents' Sunset Arms boarding house.
'SpongeBob SquarePants' was created by this marine-biologist-turned-animator.
This 1998 Nicktoon starred a conjoined cat and dog sharing a single body.
'The Ren & Stimpy Show' was created by this controversial Canadian animator.
This Texas capital is famous for its music scene and 'Keep It Weird' slogan.
Washington State's capital sits on Puget Sound, far smaller than nearby Seattle.
This Connecticut capital, a major insurance hub, lies on the Connecticut River.
Vermont's capital is the least-populous state capital in the U.S.
Maine's capital sits on the Kennebec River and shares a name with a Roman empress.
This Spider-Man foe is scientist Otto Octavius, fused to four mechanical arms.
This umbrella-wielding Batman villain is the portly Oswald Cobblepot.
This masked monarch of Latveria is the Fantastic Four's archenemy.
This Holocaust survivor and X-Men antagonist controls magnetism.
This towering crime boss, the rotund Wilson Fisk, menaces Daredevil and Spider-Man.
In chess, this term describes a king under inescapable attack, ending the game.
In Scrabble, playing all seven of your tiles at once earns this 50-point bonus.
In Monopoly, this corner square lets a player merely pass through with no penalty.
In contract bridge, winning all thirteen tricks is called this.
In Risk, holding an entire one of these landmasses grants bonus armies each turn.

final.clue: Founded in 1960 in Ypsilanti, Michigan, this chain once promised delivery in '30 minutes or it's free.'
